decisions.title: Creation uses exactly one current policy
decisions.decision: Each resource kind has exactly one non-revoked CurrentCreation policy in an authenticated snapshot; every created resource must use its exact policy identity.
rejected_alternatives: allow any accepted predecessor to create
allow semantic-content equivalence instead of exact identity
caller-selected creation policy

decisions.title: Predecessors are read/consume compatibility only
decisions.decision: AcceptedPredecessor policies may validate existing resources for reference or consumption, but confer no creation, mint, burn, reward, transformation, or verifier fallback authority by disposition alone.
rejected_alternatives: current policy only for every role
predecessor creation during a grace window

decisions.title: Hard revocation prioritizes containment
decisions.decision: HardRevoked policies authorize no normal creation, reference, consumption, logic, transformation, authority, or DA path from the effective snapshot onward. Live resources may be stranded; recovery requires a separate governed rule.
rejected_alternatives: revocation affects creation only
automatic migration on revocation
facts remain valid until their original expiry

decisions.title: Uncommitted authority is exact-context-bound
decisions.decision: Any change to pre-state root, state version, policy root, logical epoch, ordering context, crypto suite, or accumulator profile invalidates all uncommitted prevalidation results, facts, drafts, admissions, and plans from the old context.
rejected_alternatives: reuse when policy contents appear equivalent
bind only state version
bind wall-clock time instead of authenticated logical context

decisions.title: Verifier facts bind class, snapshot, release, and statement
decisions.decision: A sealed fact is usable only for its exact lifecycle class, parent binding, claim and child statement, policy snapshot, validation context, verifier policy, program/key, verifier release, authenticated output, and validity window. Production authority accepts only Production-mode policies.
rejected_alternatives: fact authority from policy ID equality alone
Development or Test receipts accepted under matching bytes
admission and postcommit receipts share one fact type

decisions.title: All durable effects are explicit and journal-bound
decisions.decision: Finalization derives one bounded CommitEffects descriptor covering active-resource deletes/inserts, nullifiers, transition replay records, rewards/escrow, outbox, audit records, and total storage bytes. The next journal schema commits its root and counts.
rejected_alternatives: storage adapter adds implicit writes
outbox and rewards remain outside the journal commitment
caller-supplied effect totals are authoritative

decisions.title: Commit linearizes on one snapshot CAS and atomic write
decisions.decision: A new commit atomically compares machine-state root, state version, and validation-context hash, rechecks policy-selected admission, and applies every declared effect plus replay record and accepted journal in one transactional or refinement-equivalent operation.
rejected_alternatives: separate non-atomic freshness checks
nullifier committed before value effects
journal accepted before durability

decisions.title: Exact retry returns prior success without reapplication
decisions.decision: When a transition record already exists and transition identity, journal hash, and effects root exactly match the plan, commit returns AlreadyCommitted(existing AcceptedJournal) and performs no write.
rejected_alternatives: reject every retry
apply again when the context is still fresh
return success based on transition ID alone

decisions.title: Mismatched replay identity is a conflict
decisions.decision: An existing transition ID with a different journal hash, effects root, or semantic identity rejects as TransitionReplayConflict and never becomes idempotent success.
rejected_alternatives: first writer silently wins for all later contents
last writer overwrites the replay record

decisions.title: Admission and postcommit recursion are different authority classes
decisions.decision: Admission binds a JournalDraft before commit; postcommit aggregation binds an AcceptedJournal after durable commit. Neither can substitute for the other.
rejected_alternatives: one generic recursive receipt type
postcommit proof retroactively authorizes a transition
admission proof implies durability

decisions.title: External delivery remains at-least-once
decisions.decision: ZRM may atomically persist an outbox entry derived from TransitionId, but external delivery is at-least-once unless the receiver supplies an idempotency contract. No exact-once external-delivery claim is made.
rejected_alternatives: treat network send as part of database atomicity
drop messages after uncertain acknowledgement

decisions.title: Policy rollback is a new version, never ABA
decisions.decision: Reactivating earlier policy contents is a new governance update with a new state version and validation context. State version never decrements and an old snapshot tuple is never reused.
rejected_alternatives: restore the old root and version
allow outstanding plans to resume after rollback

non_claims: not approved protocol semantics until independent review and maintainer decision
no canonical byte or hash freeze for new objects
no Rust implementation or refinement proof
no automatic recovery for hard-revoked resources
